data.description:
## What
Read-only derivations over the existing `REVIEW_RESULT`/`COMPLETION_VERDICT` sidecars for gate-economics + env-diagnosis metrics: LLM cost proxy (`llm_calls`, latency), cost-per-accepted-change, finding decisions / false-positive proxy, first-pass verification, and env-diagnosis intervals (ERROR -> next PASS).

## Files
- `src/rebar/metrics/sidecar_metrics.py` — new: reader over the sidecars, registered in `src/rebar/metrics/registry.py`.
- `tests/unit/metrics/test_sidecar_metrics.py` — new.

## Approach
The sidecars already persist per-run `metrics{llm_calls,det_ms,llm_ms,total_ms}`, finding decisions, and pass/fail verdicts, plus the additive `ERROR` outcome — so these are pure read-side derivations: cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes; env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket. Read-only; nothing written.

## Tests
- Unit (real sidecar fixtures): cost-per-accepted-change over 3 attempts with 1 accepted returns summed llm_calls / 1; an ERROR paired with a later PASS yields a diagnosis duration equal to the timestamp delta.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/unit/metrics/test_sidecar_metrics.py` passes: an ERROR row paired with a later PASS on the same ticket yields a diagnosis-interval duration equal to the fixture timestamp delta (`assert dur == expected`).
- [ ] cost-per-accepted-change over a fixture of 3 attempts (llm_calls 4,4,4) with 1 PASS returns 12 — unit test asserts the exact value.
data.title: Gate-sidecar reader (gate-economics + env-diagnosis metrics)
